name: Spring 6 and Spring Boot 3 Modern API Development
summary: Study graph for modern API development with Spring 6 and Spring Boot 3 across REST, reactive APIs, security, testing, deployment, gRPC, observability, and GraphQL.
aliases: Modern API Development with Spring and Spring Boot

name: RESTful Web Services
summary: Foundation section for REST API resource design, HTTP semantics, HATEOAS, caching, Spring REST implementation, OpenAPI, business logic, and reactive migration.

name: Security UI Test Deploy
summary: Section that extends REST services with security, UI consumption, API testing, and container/Kubernetes deployment.

name: gRPC Observability GraphQL
summary: Section for alternative API styles and operations: gRPC, service logging/tracing, and GraphQL implementation and testing.

name: REST API
summary: HTTP-based API style centered on resources, URIs, methods, status codes, stateless requests, and hypermedia-driven navigation.

name: Resource and URI Design
summary: REST endpoints should identify resources with stable URI paths, using nouns for resources rather than RPC-style verb endpoints.

name: HTTP Methods and Status Codes
summary: HTTP methods express resource operations and status codes communicate request outcomes, including client, auth, and server error cases.

name: HATEOAS
summary: Hypermedia links guide clients through available application state transitions and distinguish richer REST usage from RPC-style endpoints.

name: ETag and HTTP Caching
summary: ETag and related headers let clients validate cached representations and avoid unnecessary full responses when a resource has not changed.

name: API Versioning
summary: Versioning is part of REST API evolution so clients can adapt to changes without breaking existing integrations abruptly.

name: Spring Framework
summary: Java framework providing IoC, DI, AOP, MVC components, and a container model for building REST, gRPC, and GraphQL APIs.

name: Spring Boot
summary: Spring application runtime and packaging approach that simplifies configuration, dependency setup, and service execution for modern API applications.

name: IoC and Dependency Injection
summary: Spring's IoC container creates and wires beans so application code can depend on abstractions instead of constructing concrete collaborators directly.

name: Spring Bean
summary: Container-managed object created, assembled, scoped, initialized, and destroyed according to Spring metadata and lifecycle rules.

name: Spring Controller
summary: Spring MVC controller layer adapts HTTP requests and responses, commonly through REST controller endpoints.

name: Service Component
summary: Spring service component hosts business logic and coordinates repositories or collaborators behind controller endpoints.

name: Repository Component
summary: Spring repository component encapsulates persistence access, commonly behind service logic and JPA-backed storage.

name: OpenAPI Design-First Workflow
summary: API is specified with OAS before implementation, then the specification drives generated interfaces, documentation, and implementation work.

name: OpenAPI Code Generation
summary: OpenAPI/Swagger tooling can generate Spring-facing API interfaces and support a design-first implementation workflow.

name: Global Exception Handler
summary: A global exception handler centralizes API error handling so controllers can return consistent failure responses.

name: JPA Persistence
summary: JPA-backed database configuration and repository implementation persist application data behind Spring repository components.

name: Reactive API
summary: Asynchronous and non-blocking API style that uses reactive streams and pipelines rather than per-request blocking thread flow.

name: Spring WebFlux
summary: Spring reactive web framework that adapts publishers to HTTP handling and supports annotation or functional endpoint styles.

name: Reactive Streams
summary: Publisher-subscriber stream model where demand and backpressure control how data is pushed through an asynchronous pipeline.

name: Mono and Flux
summary: Reactor types used as wrappers for single or multiple asynchronous values in Spring WebFlux controllers and pipelines.

name: R2DBC and Reactive Persistence
summary: Reactive persistence boundary for non-blocking relational database access when the whole controller-to-database pipeline must remain reactive.

name: Spring Security
summary: Security framework used to protect REST endpoints with authentication, authorization, CORS, CSRF, and filter-chain based request handling.

name: JWT Access Token
summary: JSON Web Token used as a stateless access token for protected REST URLs and role-bearing authorization claims.

name: Refresh Token
summary: Token used to request a new access token after the JWT expires, allowing logout and token renewal flows to be handled separately.

name: OAuth2 Resource Server
summary: Spring Security resource-server approach that uses bearer-token authentication filters while still requiring token-issuing endpoints.

name: Roles and Authorization
summary: Role-based authorization grants access to selected REST endpoints only when the authenticated user has the required role or authority.

name: API Testing
summary: API quality is verified through manual calls and automated tests that can run as part of the build.

name: Unit and Integration Testing
summary: Unit and integration tests cover code behavior at different levels, with assertions and build integration used to keep API changes reliable.

name: Docker Containerization
summary: Application can be packaged as a Docker image so the service and runtime dependencies can be deployed consistently.

name: Kubernetes Deployment
summary: Containerized service can be deployed to a Kubernetes cluster, with minikube used as a development-friendly learning target.

name: gRPC API
summary: RPC-style API protocol used for service-to-service communication, implemented with server and client projects and tested separately.

name: Protocol Buffers
summary: gRPC uses Protocol Buffers to define service contracts and message shapes for generated client and server code.

name: ELK Logging Stack
summary: Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana are used to collect, publish, and analyze service request and response logs.

name: Zipkin and Micrometer Tracing
summary: Zipkin and Micrometer support distributed tracing and API performance visibility across service calls.

name: GraphQL API
summary: GraphQL API style uses schema definitions, queries, mutations, and subscriptions to expose flexible client-driven data access.

name: GraphQL N+1 and DataLoader
summary: GraphQL N+1 query problems are addressed with DataLoader-style batching or fetching strategies in the GraphQL implementation.

name: GraphQL Subscription
summary: GraphQL subscriptions use WebSocket-oriented flows to push updates and require dedicated testing beyond query and mutation tests.

name: OCR Source Quality Caveat
summary: The private source was built from OCR output with unclear markers, so use graph summaries for guidance and avoid exact quotation claims.
